name: Thread nonce & auth_time through the authorization code (with fail-loud guards)
outcome: A nonce supplied at /authorize and a consumer-supplied auth_time survive the authorize-to-token round trip for both JWT and opaque authorization codes. If the round trip silently drops nonce/auth_time (the opaque-code persistence failure mode), the library fails loud rather than minting a security-degraded ID token.
scope: Add nonce and auth_time to the authorization request, auth code entity, and payload; propagate them through both encoders; enforce max_age freshness; and add a fail-loud guard against the opaque-code nonce-loss footgun. No ID token minting yet.
acceptance_criteria: A nonce supplied at /authorize survives to the resolved auth code payload for both JWT and opaque codes

Opaque-code nonce-loss guard (the headline fix): if OIDC is enabled and the original AuthorizationRequest carried a nonce but the resolved opaque-code payload has none at token time, the library throws OAuthException.invalidGrant('Authorization code missing nonce — consumer opaque-code repository must persist the nonce field on OAuthAuthCode'). The same guard applies to auth_time when max_age was requested. Docs alone are insufficient mitigation

max_age freshness enforcement: when /authorize received max_age and the supplied auth_time satisfies auth_time + max_age < now at token time, throws OAuthException.invalidGrant('max_age exceeded'). Presence-only checks (auth_time exists) are insufficient

Requesting max_age without supplying authTime throws an OAuthException that surfaces as an error redirect (not an unhandled exception) — the throw is from completeAuthorizationRequest, after the consumer's login UI has run
The other OIDC authorization params (prompt, loginHint, display, uiLocales, acrValues, idTokenHint) are parsed onto AuthorizationRequest but not acted on
Non-OIDC authorization requests are unaffected — the new AuthorizationRequest fields are present but always undefined
steps: AuthorizationRequest — add nonce and authTime fields plus a parsed bag of inert OIDC params (prompt, maxAge, loginHint, display, uiLocales, acrValues, idTokenHint)
OAuthAuthCode entity and PayloadAuthCode — add optional nonce and auth_time fields
AuthCodeGrant.validateAuthorizationRequest — read nonce, max_age, and the inert OIDC params from the query string when OIDC is enabled
AuthCodeGrant.completeAuthorizationRequest — set nonce and authTime on the auth code entity, throw when max_age was requested but authTime is absent (OAuthException → error redirect, not unhandled)

JwtAuthCodeEncoder and OpaqueAuthCodeEncoder — carry nonce and auth_time across the authorize-to-token round trip. For opaque codes, both come from the persisted OAuthAuthCode (the persistence requirement). For JWT codes, both ride inside the signed code

AuthCodeGrant.respondToAccessTokenRequest — after resolving the auth code payload but before minting an ID token, check the fail-loud guards: nonce-loss and max_age freshness. Throw OAuthException.invalidGrant with a message that names the underlying consumer obligation (so the failure is debuggable)
test/unit — nonce survival for both encoders; max_age freshness enforcement; the missing-authTime throw produces an error redirect
test/e2e — opaque-code nonce-loss simulation: a stub auth code repository that 'forgets' to persist nonce; assert invalid_grant is returned and no ID token is minted
notes: Opaque codes rebuild the payload from the persisted entity, so nonce/auth_time must live on OAuthAuthCode. The fail-loud guard is the security mitigation for the opaque-code persistence footgun — documented in issue 9 as a consumer obligation, but enforced here so an incorrect implementation surfaces as invalid_grant rather than as a nonce-less (replay-vulnerable) ID token. Re-pointed 3 → 2 to reflect that this is mechanical threading; the security weight lives in the guard, which is small and well-tested.
